etymology.description: 生(生) (shēng, "life") + 辶(辶) (chuò, "walk; movement") = phonetic component with a semantic influence suggesting multiplicity -> final form representing the concept of a broom-like tool made from multiple branches or straws
etymology.interpretation: The character 甡 (shēn) uses the phonetic 生(生) and the semantic 辶(辶) to convey the idea of a broom made from multiple parts, possibly alluding to the branching aspect as shown by 生(生), and movement related to use, indicated by 辶(辶). It suggests an old broom-like object.
